error.description: The human-facing reason the request was refused.

SynthesizedPackument.description: Écluse's merged-and-filtered view of a package's metadata. Versions are merged across upstreams and gated (private versions trusted, public versions admitted only by policy), and each version's `dist.tarball` is rewritten to resolve back through this proxy. Only the fields Écluse reads and transforms are modelled; every other field is relayed unchanged from the contributing upstream (the private upstream wins on a collision).

dist-tags.description: Tag to version string. `latest` is repointed to the newest surviving version after the gate.

time.description: Publish timestamps: `created`, `modified`, and one entry per version.

additionalProperties.description: A single version's manifest. Only the fields Écluse reads or transforms are modelled; the rest are relayed unchanged.

dist.description: The artifact descriptor. `tarball` is rewritten to resolve through the proxy; `integrity`/`shasum` are preserved byte-for-byte so the client's own check still holds.

shasum.description: Legacy SHA-1 digest, preserved from upstream.

tarball.description: Rewritten artifact URL, under this mount's prefix.

versions.description: Surviving versions, keyed by version string.

SynthesizedPackument.title: Synthesized packument

info.description: Which registry protocols this Écluse server speaks, and exactly what is and is not supported, per ecosystem. A capability manifest for operators and contributors — not a client-integration contract: registry clients hardcode the protocol and never read this document. Generated statically from the closed serve-route enumeration; it is not served.
info.title: Écluse capability manifest

get.description: Answered locally with `200` and an empty object; `npm ping` checks the endpoint it talks to is up, so there is no reason to round-trip upstream.

Search is a first-class documented boundary: a discovery convenience, not an install path, so Écluse returns `501` and points to the public registry's website rather than scope-creeping a filtered or pass-through search.

501.description: Not implemented: search is not supported.

get.description: Returns Écluse's merged-and-filtered packument: versions merged across upstreams and gated, each `dist.tarball` rewritten to resolve back through this proxy. With no surviving version the status follows the most recoverable cause.

403.description: Every version was withheld by policy or admission, and none survived the merge.

404.description: No such package upstream (a forwarded miss).

500.description: A permanent or internal inability to decide.

502.description: A responding upstream returned a packument for a different package.

503.description: A transient upstream or advisory condition; retry (see `Retry-After`).

parameters.description: The package name, URL-encoded; a scoped name is `@scope%2Fname`.
parameters.name: package

put.description: Relays the publish document to the configured publication target after the anti-shadowing scope guard. Écluse keys the write on the route's package name, never the document's self-reported name.

schema.description: The npm publish document, relayed to the publication target (its full shape is npm's, not re-specified here).

requestBody.description: The npm publish document (the version manifest plus the base64-encoded tarball in `_attachments`).

201.description: The publication target accepted the package (its response is relayed).

403.description: The package name is outside the configured publish scopes (anti-shadowing), or refused by policy.

405.description: Publishing is not configured (no publication target).

get.description: The artifact bytes are streamed verbatim with bounded memory; the manifest documents the media type and links out rather than re-specifying the upstream artifact protocol. The client verifies the bytes against the packument's preserved integrity digest.

403.description: Refused by policy, or by admission (a missing or below-floor integrity digest).

404.description: The upstream did not have the artifact (a forwarded miss).

500.description: A permanent or internal inability to serve.

parameters.description: The package name, URL-encoded; a scoped name is `@scope%2Fname`.
parameters.name: package

parameters.description: The artifact's on-the-wire file name, e.g. `lodash-4.17.21.tgz`.
parameters.name: filename

get.description: Any request under this mount matched by none of the routes above is denied with `404` — deny by default at the routing layer.

parameters.description: Any path under this mount matched by none of the routes above.
parameters.name: unsupportedPath

servers.description: The proxy's externally-reachable base URL; served artifact URLs resolve against it.
